customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, cooperative posture throughout. No scam skepticism, no competitive quotes mentioned, no financial vulnerability disclosed. Primary risk is appointment fragility due to wife's uncertain availability — not customer resistance.
unresolved_concerns: Wife's attendance at the 7PM Tuesday appointment is uncertain — Nicholas said 'Maybe seven. I'm not sure on her schedule' and referenced a possible clinic conflict. Carter echoed the hedge rather than reanchoring.
No 'Is that fair?' verbal handshake was obtained — appointment commitment is softer than it should be.
No-driving/at-computer anchor was not set — closer should confirm Nicholas will be at a computer for the appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Nicholas mentioned his wife is a medical student with clinic obligations — scheduling around her is a real constraint, not an excuse.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for Tuesday at 7PM (phone call).
Carter said 'we'll slot you in for an hour' — one-hour block was stated.
Carter said 'I'll go ahead and shoot you a text just with the date and time so you have it in writing' — text follow-up was promised.
closer_should_know: The wife is on title and was flagged as a required attendee, but her attendance is conditional on her clinic schedule. Nicholas said 'Maybe seven' and introduced the uncertainty himself — Carter echoed it rather than locking it down. Closer should confirm at the top of the call that both Nicholas and his wife are present before proceeding. If the wife is absent, closer should assess whether to proceed or reschedule. Additionally: Nicholas named AES Columbus as the utility and cited a projected rate hike as his primary motivation — this is his own framing and the closer should anchor the proposal to it. Bill is $200/month and has not been emotionally flipped yet — the closer has a clean opportunity to open with the rate-hike pain Nicholas already articulated.
